judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations; they are comparable, with Report 2 marginally sharper on the same-commit doc-fix rejection of deferral.

report_1.evidence: New refund provider calls should live in `medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py`... `medibill/backend/app/api/invoices.py:14-16` — imports `chargekit` (line 4), builds its own `chargekit.Client(...)`... `medibill/backend/app/workers/dunning.py:9-13`... `errors.py:6` also imports from `chargekit`, but only the error classes — that is the boundary exception the doc explicitly allows... so it is not a violation.

report_2.evidence: New refund calls should live in **`backend/app/billing/gateway.py`**... `backend/app/api/invoices.py`... `backend/app/workers/dunning.py`... The one **legitimate** exception is **`backend/app/billing/errors.py:6`**, which imports *only* the SDK's error classes

report_1.evidence: The actual serializer `InvoiceOut` (models/invoice.py:16-28) carries a sixth field, `external_ref`... migration `0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql` later added `external_ref TEXT`.

report_2.evidence: `InvoiceOut` (`models/invoice.py:16–22`) carries a sixth field, `external_ref` (line 22)... migration `0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql` added the column

report_1.evidence: make the doc edits part of the same change set that touches these areas rather than deferring them... The anti-pattern to avoid is adding refunds cleanly to the gateway while leaving the doc and the two bypassing call sites untouched

report_2.evidence: drift here is a **bug to fix now**, not a "clean up someday" item... Any doc edit lands in the **same commit** as the code change that resolves it, and the refund PR itself must update the doc in that same commit

report_1.evidence: the count is wrong. `api/webhooks.py:10-19` branches on **five** types... don't pin an exact integer in prose — that hard count is exactly what drifted. Restate it as an enumerated list that is the source of truth

report_2.evidence: The code handles **five**... The hardcoded "4" is guaranteed to drift and already has... the durable fix is to stop hardcoding a count no test guards... drop the bare count and point at `app/api/webhooks.py` as the single source of truth
